fixes_applied_before_testing.issue: submit_result's agent_address param typed as Address arrived as raw int on this runtime
fixes_applied_before_testing.fix: Changed param to str, convert internally via Address(agent_address), matching the proven add_expected_agent pattern

fixes_applied_before_testing.issue: register_task allowed expected_count=0, creating a task that can never be finalized (finalize only fires from inside submit_result)
fixes_applied_before_testing.fix: Added guard: raise UserError('expected_count must be at least 1') when expected_count == 0

tests.description: Two agents submit matching verdicts for same capability -> deterministic aggregation path

actual_result.summary: [security] high: Reentrancy risk detected in withdraw() | [security] high: Confirmed unchecked external call

tests.description: Two agents submit conflicting verdicts for same capability -> LLM aggregation via gl.eq_principle.prompt_comparative

args.summary: No issues found in static analysis

tests.expected: has_conflict=True (high vs clear) -> _llm_aggregate -> validator consensus via prompt_comparative, synthesized verdict+summary

actual_result.summary: Reentrancy risk in withdraw() takes precedence over clear static analysis, as reentrancy is a dynamic vulnerability often missed by static tools.

tests.description: Duplicate submit_result on an already-finalized task is a silent no-op
tests.expected: finalized_flags[task_id]=True already -> early return, no state change, no revert

tests.description: submit_result from an agent never added via add_expected_agent
tests.expected: revert: 'Agent is not part of this task's execution plan'

actual_result.rollback_message: Agent is not part of this task's execution plan

tests.description: submit_result on a task_id that was never registered

actual_result.note: Studio UI blocked the call client-side before submission; view-method revert surfaced immediately without a full consensus tx cycle

tests.status: PASS (behavior consistent with guard, not independently tx-verifiable via Studio UI)

tests.description: register_task called twice with the same task_id
add_expected_agent called on an already-finalized task

Three agents submit to the same task; due to a form input mistake all three landed on capability='security' (intended: 2x security + 1x liquidity) with verdicts high/high/clear -> real 3-way conflict on one capability
tests.expected: has_conflict=True (high/high/clear on same capability) -> _llm_aggregate resolves 3-way disagreement

actual_result.summary: The reviewers agree that a reentrancy issue is present and confirmed, which is a high-severity security finding. The sufficient liquidity note does not mitigate the vulnerability, so the final verdict remains high.

tests.note: Deviated from planned scenario (intended 2 capabilities) due to form input error, but incidentally validated 3-way LLM conflict resolution with majority-aware but not majority-blind reasoning

tests.description: register_task with expected_count=0, before the guard fix -> task can never be finalized since _finalize only fires inside submit_result
tests.expected_pre_fix: Task registers successfully but remains permanently unfinalized (dead task, no legal path to _finalize)
tests.fix_applied: Added guard in register_task: raise UserError('expected_count must be at least 1') when expected_count == 0

tests.description: register_task with expected_count=0 on fresh deploy with the fix applied

coverage: zero expected_count edge case (bug found and fixed)
